prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the year of our Lord two thousand and twenty-three, the scribes of the great SaaS dominion didst proclaimeth the third quarter's bounty, wherein the ethereal clouds didst beareth forth a harvest of digital scrolls numbering in the myriad, and the coffers of the realm didst swell with the golden coins of subscription fees. Lo, the wizards of data magic hath conjured a potion of growth, and the unicorns of revenue galloped forth with unbridled vigor, their manes aglow with the radiance of recurring billing. Verily, the oracles of customer lifetime value hath foreseen a future as bright as the heavens on a moonless night, and the high council of stakeholders didst rejoice, for the auguries of profit margins were most favorable. Thus, in this age of digital enchantment, the chronicler doth bear witness to the marvels of the cloud-borne empire, and doth scribe these wonders for posterity.
